ogTitle: Wild Cattle Creek vs Wiley Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wild Cattle Creek and Wiley Park.
heading: Wild Cattle Creek vs Wiley Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wild Cattle Creek, New South Wales and Wiley Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wiley Park has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Wild Cattle Creek (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wild Cattle Creek or Wiley Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wild Cattle Creek has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Wiley Park scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wild Cattle Creek scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Wiley Park scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wild Cattle Creek or Wiley Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wiley Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
